project.name: ruvector slice 6c — rvm + rvlite
project.description: RVM: Coherence-native microhypervisor for edge computing and multi-agent systems; rvlite: standalone WebAssembly vector database with SQL/SPARQL/Cypher interfaces. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for rvlite v0.3.0 — a standalone vector database (SQL/SPARQL/Cypher) compiled to WASM with cdylib+rlib crate types, wiring ruvector-core, wasm-bindgen, IndexedDB web-sys features, and optional RVF runtime/SQL feature flags.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy feature-gating with optional dependencies (rvf-runtime, fs2, sqlparser) supports both browser-WASM and native-RVF backends from one crate.

nodes.name: GETRANDOM_RESOLUTION_STRATEGY.md
nodes.summary: Engineering note documenting the strategy for resolving getrandom backend conflicts in the rvlite WASM build, covering wasm-bindgen vs js feature flags and per-target cfg branches across 28 sections.

nodes.name: INDEX.md
nodes.summary: Index for the rvlite dashboard example bundle, mapping the React+Vite SPA entry points, helper scripts, and learning-engine hooks that demonstrate the standalone vector DB.

nodes.name: apply-filter-builder.sh
nodes.summary: Shell helper that patches the dashboard App.tsx to wire in the FilterBuilder UI component, automating a repeatable code-mod for the rvlite example.

nodes.name: filter-helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the `useFilterConditionHelpers` hook factory that builds add/remove/update callbacks for the dashboard's filter-condition state and syncs them to a JSON representation.

nodes.name: useFilterConditionHelpers
nodes.summary: Hook factory returning add/remove/update/toggle helpers that mutate filter-condition arrays and serialize them to JSON for the dashboard query builder.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root React component for the rvlite dashboard demo — a 4000+ line single-file SPA orchestrating SQL/SPARQL/Cypher query consoles, metrics panels, filter builder, and learning-engine integration against the WASM-loaded rvlite database.
nodes.languageNotes: Monolithic App component (over 3600 lines) handles routing, state, query execution, and rendering; flagged as complex due to size — typical pattern for example/demo apps but unusual for production code.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Main App functional component orchestrating the dashboard layout, query consoles, metric streaming, and the learning-engine panel for the rvlite demo.

nodes.name: generateMetrics
nodes.summary: Generates a fresh batch of synthetic dashboard metrics (timings, counters) for the demo when live data isn't available.

nodes.name: getErrorMessage
nodes.summary: Normalizes assorted error shapes (Error, WASM panic, string, object) into a human-readable string for the dashboard's error banner.

nodes.name: useLearning.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the LearningEngine class plus a React `useLearning` hook that records query executions, extracts patterns, computes metrics, generates suggestions/insights, and persists state to localStorage for the dashboard's adaptive-assist panel.

nodes.name: LearningEngine
nodes.summary: In-memory engine that derives query patterns from execution history, scores them with feedback, emits suggestions/insights, and serializes to/from localStorage.

nodes.name: useLearning
nodes.summary: React hook exposing the singleton LearningEngine to dashboard components — wraps recordExecution/feedback, exposes reactive metrics, and triggers re-renders on engine state changes.

nodes.name: getLearningEngine
nodes.summary: Singleton accessor that lazily constructs and returns the shared LearningEngine instance for the dashboard.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite configuration for the rvlite dashboard example — wires the React plugin, dev server settings, and WASM asset handling for loading the rvlite library at runtime.

nodes.name: lib_sql.rs
nodes.summary: Library entry exposing the rvlite SQL module surface — re-exports the parser/executor types from `src/sql/mod.rs` for downstream wasm-bindgen wrappers.

nodes.name: tests.rs
nodes.summary: Unit test suite for the rvlite SQL engine covering parser round-trips, CRUD execution, and edge-case error handling against the sql module.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: Minimal GitHub Actions CI workflow for the rvm (RuVector Machine) workspace that runs cargo check/test on pushes and pull requests.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the rvm-boot crate — the bare-metal bootstrap layer of the RuVector Machine agent runtime, wiring HAL, memory, and scheduler crates for no_std boot.

nodes.name: error.rs
nodes.summary: Defines `CapError` and `ProofError` no_std error enums for the rvm-cap (capabilities) crate, with manual Display impls and From conversions wiring proof failures into capability failures.

nodes.name: CapError
nodes.summary: Enumerates capability-system failure modes (revoked, expired, unauthorized, proof-failure) with a no_std-compatible Display impl.

nodes.name: ProofError
nodes.summary: Enumerates witness/proof verification failures used by the capability layer when a presented proof cannot be validated.

nodes.name: bridge.rs
nodes.summary: Bridges the rvm-coherence engine to the wider RuVector ecosystem via the MinCutBackend and CoherenceBackend traits — supplies built-in Stoer-Wagner/ratio backends plus stubs that will delegate to the ruvector crates once they gain no_std support.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses fixed-size arrays (e.g. `[Option<PartitionId>; 32]`) to avoid heap allocation — required because the crate must run in no_std contexts.

nodes.name: BackendMinCutResult
nodes.summary: Heap-free result struct returned by mincut backends — carries left/right partition arrays, total cut weight, and a within-budget flag.

nodes.name: MinCutBackend
nodes.summary: Trait abstracting a minimum-cut computation backend, decoupling the engine from a specific algorithm or external implementation.

nodes.name: BuiltinMinCut
nodes.summary: Built-in Stoer-Wagner mincut backend that ships with rvm-coherence and works without any external crate.

nodes.name: RuVectorMinCut
nodes.summary: Feature-gated mincut backend stub that will delegate to the external ruvector-mincut crate once it gains no_std support.

nodes.name: CoherenceBackend
nodes.summary: Trait abstracting partition coherence scoring backends used by the rvm-coherence engine.

nodes.name: BuiltinCoherence
nodes.summary: Built-in coherence backend using the simple ratio-based scoring shipped with rvm-coherence.

nodes.name: SpectralCoherence
nodes.summary: Feature-gated spectral coherence backend stub intended to delegate to ruvector-coherence once no_std support lands.

nodes.name: interrupts.rs
nodes.summary: ARM aarch64 HAL implementation of the GIC (Generic Interrupt Controller) — provides MMIO read/write primitives plus an Aarch64Gic type implementing init/enable/disable/ack/EOI for the rvm scheduler's interrupt path.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses raw volatile MMIO writes to the GIC distributor and CPU interface registers — typical bare-metal pattern.

nodes.name: Aarch64Gic
nodes.summary: Aarch64 Generic Interrupt Controller driver type implementing init/enable/disable/acknowledge/end_of_interrupt against the GICv2 MMIO interface.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the rvm-memory crate — the no_std memory-region and tier management layer underpinning the RVM agent runtime's allocator and placement policy.

nodes.name: comm_edge.rs
nodes.summary: Defines `CommEdgeId` newtype and `CommEdge` struct representing inter-partition communication links — the basic edge type the coherence and scheduler subsystems reason over.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the rvm-proof crate — witness/attestation primitives including HMAC signing and TEE-backed signers used by the witness chain.

nodes.name: tee_signer.rs
nodes.summary: Implements `TeeWitnessSigner`, a witness signer that wraps an HMAC signer with TEE attestation — derives report_data from the witness digest, self-attests via the TEE provider, and exposes verify/sign trait impls for the proof pipeline.

nodes.name: TeeWitnessSigner
nodes.summary: Witness signer combining an HMAC inner signer with a TEE provider+verifier — binds attestation reports to witness digests and exposes sign/verify/signer_id for the witness chain.

nodes.name: scheduler.rs
nodes.summary: Core RVM scheduler implementing per-CPU run queues, epoch ticking, degraded-mode entry/exit, and partition enqueue/dispatch keyed by deadline urgency and cut pressure — the heart of the agent runtime's execution dispatch.

nodes.name: Scheduler
nodes.summary: Top-level scheduler holding per-CPU run queues, current mode/epoch, and degraded-mode flag — exposes enqueue, switch_next, tick_epoch, and queue-introspection methods to the rest of the RVM kernel.

nodes.name: RunQueueEntry
nodes.summary: Run queue entry bundling a partition id, deadline urgency, and cut pressure for priority ordering inside the scheduler.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the rvm-types crate — the foundational shared-types crate (IDs, scores, memory regions) referenced by every other rvm-* crate in the workspace.

nodes.name: memory.rs
nodes.summary: Defines shared memory-related types — `OwnedRegionId`, `MemoryTier`, `RegionPolicy`, `RegionPlacementWeights`, and `MemoryRegion` — that the memory, scheduler, and partition crates all consume.

nodes.name: MemoryRegion
nodes.summary: Describes a single memory region — its id, tier, policy, and placement weights — used by the allocator and placement engine.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Root of the rvm-wasm crate — defines WASM module state/section types and a `validate_module` parser that performs a header+LEB128 scan to validate guest agent modules before loading them into the RVM runtime.
nodes.languageNotes: Hand-rolled WASM section walker using LEB128 decoding — avoids pulling in a full WASM parser crate to keep no_std and code size small.

nodes.name: WasmModuleInfo
nodes.summary: Summary of a parsed WASM module — counts of sections, imports, exports — collected during validation.

nodes.name: WasmSectionId
nodes.summary: Enumerates WASM binary section ids (Type, Import, Function, ... Data) with a `from_u8` decoder used by the validator.

nodes.name: WasmValidationResult
nodes.summary: Result of validating a WASM agent module — carries the parsed ModuleInfo and any validation diagnostics.

nodes.name: replay.rs
nodes.summary: Witness-chain replay utilities — verifies hash chain integrity across recorded action records and exposes query helpers (by partition, action kind, time range) for auditors.

nodes.name: verify_chain
nodes.summary: Verifies the hash-chain integrity of a slice of witness records, returning a ChainIntegrityError at the first inconsistency.

nodes.name: ChainIntegrityError
nodes.summary: Enumerates witness-chain replay failures (broken link, bad hash, out-of-order timestamps).

nodes.name: README.md

nodes.summary: React hook that loads the RvLite WASM module, wraps its C-style API in a typed JS object, and exposes vector/SQL/Cypher/SPARQL operations with persisted IndexedDB storage and BBS-style boot logging.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines dynamic ESM import of generated wasm-bindgen glue with useCallback-wrapped operations and useEffect bootstrap.

nodes.name: createWasmWrapper
nodes.summary: Wraps a raw wasm-bindgen RvLite handle into a typed object exposing vector CRUD, SQL/Cypher/SPARQL query, RDF triple, and IndexedDB persistence methods.

nodes.name: useRvLite
nodes.summary: Main React hook that initializes the RvLite WASM database, manages loading/ready/error state, and returns memoized callbacks for insert/search/query/save/export/import/clear operations plus a dimension-change reset path.

nodes.name: demo.html
nodes.summary: Self-contained 751-line HTML demo page that exercises RvLite's WASM API from a single page without any build step.

nodes.name: ast.rs
nodes.summary: SPARQL 1.1 abstract syntax tree definitions for the RvLite query engine, modeling queries (SELECT/CONSTRUCT/ASK/DESCRIBE), graph/triple patterns, RDF terms, expressions, aggregates, solution modifiers, and update operations.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses idiomatic Rust enums to model SPARQL grammar with constructor helpers on each variant.

nodes.name: SparqlQuery
nodes.summary: Top-level SPARQL query node carrying base IRI, prefix bindings, and the query body.

nodes.name: SelectQuery
nodes.summary: AST node for SPARQL SELECT queries, holding projection, dataset clauses, WHERE pattern, and solution modifiers.

nodes.name: ConstructQuery
nodes.summary: AST node for SPARQL CONSTRUCT queries that produce RDF triples from a template plus WHERE pattern.

nodes.name: GraphPattern
nodes.summary: Enum modeling all SPARQL graph pattern shapes including BGP, OPTIONAL, UNION, GRAPH, FILTER, MINUS, BIND, and SERVICE.

nodes.name: TriplePattern
nodes.summary: Subject/predicate/object triple pattern used inside basic graph patterns.

nodes.name: RdfTerm
nodes.summary: RDF term enum covering IRI, literal, and blank node values, with helper constructors and type predicates.

nodes.name: Iri
nodes.summary: IRI newtype with constructors for common RDF/RDFS/XSD vocabulary IRIs used during SPARQL evaluation.

nodes.name: Literal
nodes.summary: RDF literal value with constructors for simple, typed, and language-tagged forms plus numeric/boolean accessors.

nodes.name: Expression
nodes.summary: SPARQL expression enum covering variables, terms, literals, binary/unary ops, function calls, aggregates, and built-in predicates.

nodes.name: Aggregate
nodes.summary: SPARQL aggregate enum (COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX, GROUP_CONCAT, SAMPLE) tracking DISTINCT and arguments.

nodes.name: SolutionModifier
nodes.summary: ORDER BY / LIMIT / OFFSET / HAVING combination applied to a SPARQL query result set.

nodes.name: UpdateOperation
nodes.summary: SPARQL Update operation enum (INSERT DATA, DELETE DATA, MODIFY, LOAD, CLEAR, CREATE, DROP, COPY, MOVE, ADD).

nodes.name: epoch.rs
nodes.summary: Epoch tracking and reconciliation logic that compares RVF persistence epoch with metadata epoch and selects rollback/replay/reconcile actions on storage open.
nodes.languageNotes: Drives crash-recovery decisions deterministically from compare_epochs/reconcile_action.

nodes.name: Epoch
nodes.summary: Monotonic epoch counter newtype with next() and value() helpers.

nodes.name: ReconcileAction
nodes.summary: Enum describing the action to take when reconciling RVF and metadata epochs (no-op, replay, rollback, error).

nodes.name: EpochTracker
nodes.summary: Tracks current epoch and exposes begin_write/commit/force_set plus check_and_reconcile helpers for storage layers.

nodes.name: compare_epochs
nodes.summary: Classifies the relationship between an RVF epoch and a metadata epoch into an EpochState (consistent/ahead/behind).

nodes.name: reconcile_action
nodes.summary: Maps an EpochState plus context into a concrete ReconcileAction (replay forward, rollback, log inconsistency).

nodes.name: reconcile
nodes.summary: Top-level entry that composes compare_epochs and reconcile_action to produce a recovery decision given both epochs.

nodes.name: check_and_reconcile
nodes.summary: EpochTracker method that invokes reconcile() and applies the resulting action, updating internal epoch state.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo workspace manifest for RVM, the coherence-native microhypervisor, listing 13 internal crates (types, hal, cap, witness, proof, partition, sched, memory, coherence, boot, wasm, security, kernel) and pinning no_std-friendly dependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses workspace.dependencies path entries to link the 13 sibling crates and configures release profile with lto=fat and panic=abort for embedded targets.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Crate-level README for rvm-boot describing the cold-boot sequence, secure boot, and partition bootstrap responsibilities of the RVM hypervisor boot module.

nodes.name: grant.rs
nodes.summary: Capability grant validation for RVM: enforces grant-depth bounds, rights monotonicity, badge propagation, and epoch freshness when one capability is derived from another.

nodes.name: GrantPolicy
nodes.summary: Policy configuration controlling capability grant constraints such as maximum delegation depth.

nodes.name: validate_grant
nodes.summary: Validates a derived capability against its source: checks epoch freshness, depth limits, requested rights subset, and badge consistency before authorizing the new grant.

nodes.name: engine.rs
nodes.summary: Central CoherenceEngine that combines a partition communication graph with adaptive pressure analysis and mincut/coherence backends to recommend partition merge/split/migration decisions each scheduling tick.
nodes.languageNotes: Wires together graph, adaptive, bridge, and pressure modules behind pluggable backends (default vs ruvector).

nodes.name: CoherenceEngine
nodes.summary: Stateful engine that tracks partitions, communication graph, and adaptive pressure to drive coherence-based scheduling decisions.

nodes.name: CoherenceDecision
nodes.summary: Enum of recommended actions (merge, split, migrate, no-op) emitted by the coherence engine for downstream schedulers.

nodes.name: tick
nodes.summary: Advances the coherence engine one epoch by feeding the current CPU-load reading into the adaptive pressure model.

nodes.name: recommend
nodes.summary: Core decision routine that produces a CoherenceDecision per partition using mincut analysis, communication weights, and adaptive pressure thresholds.

nodes.name: record_communication
nodes.summary: Records an inter-partition communication event with a weight, updating the underlying coherence graph.

nodes.name: add_partition
nodes.summary: Registers a new partition with the engine, allocating graph/pressure state for it.

nodes.name: remove_partition
nodes.summary: Removes a partition and tears down its graph/pressure entries.

nodes.name: mmu.rs
nodes.summary: ARMv8-A Stage-2 MMU support for the RVM hypervisor: builds 2 MiB block page tables, identity-maps RAM/devices, installs VTTBR_EL2, and performs guest→host address translation.
nodes.languageNotes: Targets bare-metal aarch64 with no_std-style page-table layout and VMID tagging.

nodes.name: Stage2PageTable
nodes.summary: Owned stage-2 page-table structure that allocates L1/L2 tables and exposes 2 MiB block mapping primitives.

nodes.name: Aarch64Mmu
nodes.summary: Aarch64Mmu binds a Stage2PageTable to a VMID and provides install/map_page/unmap_page/translate operations for a guest partition.

nodes.name: map_2mb_block
nodes.summary: Installs a 2 MiB stage-2 block descriptor mapping an intermediate-physical address to a physical address with the given memory attributes.

nodes.name: identity_map_ram
nodes.summary: Identity-maps a region of RAM at 2 MiB granularity into the stage-2 page table.

nodes.name: identity_map_devices
nodes.summary: Identity-maps the MMIO/device region with device memory attributes for guest pass-through.

nodes.name: alloc_l2_for
nodes.summary: Allocates an L2 table for a given L1 index when a finer-grain mapping is needed.

nodes.name: install
nodes.summary: Programs VTTBR_EL2 with this MMU's stage-2 page-table base and VMID and issues TLB invalidation.

nodes.name: map_page
nodes.summary: Maps a guest IPA to a host physical address through the stage-2 page table.

nodes.name: unmap_page
nodes.summary: Removes a guest IPA mapping and invalidates the corresponding TLB entry.

nodes.name: translate
nodes.summary: Software walks the stage-2 tables to translate a guest IPA into a host physical address.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Crate README describing the rvm-memory module's role in partition memory allocation, mapping, and accounting within RVM.

nodes.name: device.rs
nodes.summary: Device-lease manager that registers MMIO/PCI devices and grants epoch-bounded leases to partitions with capability-hash binding and explicit revoke/expire paths.

nodes.name: DeviceInfo
nodes.summary: Static description of a leasable device (id, kind, MMIO range, IRQ).

nodes.name: ActiveLease
nodes.summary: Tracks an active device lease: holder partition, granted epoch, expiry epoch, and capability hash.

nodes.name: DeviceLeaseManager
nodes.summary: Central registry that owns device records and the set of active leases, exposing grant/revoke/expire/inspect APIs.

nodes.name: grant_lease
nodes.summary: Grants a device to a partition for a bounded number of epochs, refusing the grant if the device is already leased or unknown.

nodes.name: revoke_lease
nodes.summary: Explicitly revokes an outstanding device lease, freeing the device for re-grant.

nodes.name: check_lease
nodes.summary: Validates that a lease is still active at the current epoch and returns its holder.

nodes.name: expire_leases
nodes.summary: Sweeps active leases at a given current epoch, expiring any whose deadline has passed and returning the released devices.

nodes.name: register_device
nodes.summary: Registers a new DeviceInfo with the manager so it becomes available for leasing.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Crate README for rvm-proof outlining its role in TEE attestation verification, witness signing, and constant-time cryptographic helpers.

nodes.name: tee_verifier.rs
nodes.summary: Software TEE quote verifier that checks signer identity, freshness against current/collateral epochs, expected measurement, and report data using constant-time comparisons.

nodes.name: SoftwareTeeVerifier
nodes.summary: Verifier configuration holding the trusted signer key, current epoch, and collateral expiry epoch.

nodes.name: verify_quote
nodes.summary: Verifies a TEE quote: checks epoch freshness against collateral expiry, validates expected measurement and report data, and authenticates the signer with constant-time comparison.

nodes.name: set_epoch
nodes.summary: Updates the verifier's current epoch and collateral expiry settings.

nodes.name: smp.rs
nodes.summary: SMP coordinator for RVM that tracks per-CPU state (offline/idle/running), maps partitions to CPUs, and provides idle-CPU search plus rebalance hints for the scheduler.

nodes.name: CpuState
nodes.summary: Per-CPU state enum (offline, idle, running a partition) used by the SMP coordinator.

nodes.name: SmpCoordinator
nodes.summary: Owns the CPU state vector and partition-affinity bookkeeping for an SMP system.

nodes.name: bring_online
nodes.summary: Transitions a CPU from offline to idle, making it available to the scheduler.

nodes.name: take_offline
nodes.summary: Releases any partition affinity and marks a CPU offline so the scheduler will skip it.

nodes.name: assign_partition
nodes.summary: Binds an idle CPU to a partition, recording affinity for future rebalancing.

nodes.name: rebalance_hint
nodes.summary: Computes a coarse rebalance signal indicating whether load distribution is uneven across CPUs.

nodes.name: new
nodes.summary: Constructs a SmpCoordinator with the given CPU count, all initially offline.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Crate README for rvm-types, the foundational type definitions (partition identifiers, states, capabilities) shared across the RVM workspace.

nodes.name: partition.rs
nodes.summary: Shared partition type primitives: PartitionState lifecycle enum, PartitionType classifier, and PartitionConfig descriptor used throughout RVM crates.

nodes.name: PartitionState
nodes.summary: Lifecycle enum for a partition (Created/Running/Suspended/Terminated/etc).

nodes.name: PartitionType
nodes.summary: Classifier enum distinguishing partition kinds (WASM agent, native, system, etc.).

nodes.name: PartitionConfig
nodes.summary: Construction-time partition descriptor carrying memory/CPU/security parameters used when creating a partition.

nodes.name: migration.rs
nodes.summary: WASM agent migration state machine: drives Begin → CapturedState → Transferred → Resumed transitions with timestamping, witness-log integration, and abort handling.

nodes.name: MigrationPlan
nodes.summary: Static descriptor of a WASM agent migration (agent id, source/target partitions, deadline).

nodes.name: MigrationState
nodes.summary: Migration state enum with next/is_terminal helpers for stepping through the migration lifecycle.

nodes.name: MigrationTracker
nodes.summary: Per-migration tracker that owns the current state, timestamps, and witness-log emissions across begin/advance/abort.

nodes.name: begin
nodes.summary: Starts a new migration from a plan, capturing the start timestamp and initial state.

nodes.name: advance
nodes.summary: Advances the migration state machine one step, emitting a witness-log record and rejecting transitions when the deadline has passed.

nodes.name: abort
nodes.summary: Aborts an in-flight migration and emits a corresponding witness-log record.

nodes.name: emit_migration_witness
nodes.summary: Helper that appends a migration event (begin/advance/abort with plan context) to the witness log.

nodes.name: signer.rs
nodes.summary: Witness signer abstractions for RVM: defines the WitnessSigner trait, a Null/Strict default, and an HMAC-SHA256 based signer with deterministic record serialization and FNV-1a digest fallback.

nodes.name: WitnessSigner
nodes.summary: Trait that abstracts witness signing: compute_signature and verify on a record.

nodes.name: NullSigner
nodes.summary: Test-only signer that produces empty signatures and accepts all inputs.

nodes.name: StrictSigner
nodes.summary: Signer wrapper that refuses to verify unsigned records, enforcing presence of authentic signatures.

nodes.name: HmacWitnessSigner
nodes.summary: HMAC-SHA256 witness signer producing deterministic 32-byte tags over canonical record serializations.

nodes.name: record_to_bytes
nodes.summary: Canonically serializes a witness record into a byte buffer for signing/verification.

nodes.name: fnv1a_64
nodes.summary: FNV-1a 64-bit hash used as a lightweight non-cryptographic digest for record identifiers.

nodes.name: compute_signature
nodes.summary: HmacWitnessSigner implementation that HMAC-SHA256s the canonical record bytes under the configured key.

nodes.name: verify
nodes.summary: Verifies a record's HMAC signature in constant time using the subtle crate.

nodes.name: build.rs
nodes.summary: Cargo build script that configures the getrandom WASM backend when targeting wasm32, enabling rvlite to run in browser-style environments.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses cargo:rustc-env and rustc-cfg directives to propagate compile-time configuration.

nodes.summary: Reference of copy-pasteable code snippets for wiring the FilterBuilder component into the dashboard at specific line offsets.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet for the dashboard importing Tailwind and HeroUI plugins, plus base typography and custom scrollbar styling.

nodes.name: env-polyfill.js
nodes.summary: JavaScript polyfill providing host-side simsimd vector math (cosine, dot, L2-squared) for WASM-loaded rvlite modules that import these as environment functions.

nodes.name: executor.rs
nodes.summary: SPARQL query executor evaluating SELECT/CONSTRUCT/ASK/DESCRIBE/UPDATE queries against an rvlite triple store, including BGP matching, joins, filters, expressions, and result projection.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements full RDF algebra: BGP matching, left-join (OPTIONAL), MINUS, filter, and binary/unary expression evaluation.

nodes.name: execute_sparql
nodes.summary: Top-level dispatch that parses a SPARQL query string and routes to the appropriate execution path (SELECT/CONSTRUCT/ASK/DESCRIBE/UPDATE).

nodes.name: project_solutions
nodes.summary: Applies SPARQL projection rules to candidate solutions, expanding star selectors, evaluating bound expressions, and renaming variables per the projection spec.

nodes.name: evaluate_graph_pattern
nodes.summary: Recursively evaluates SPARQL graph patterns (BGP, Join, LeftJoin, Union, Minus, Filter, Extend) producing solution sequences.

nodes.name: evaluate_bgp
nodes.summary: Evaluates a basic graph pattern by iteratively matching triple patterns and accumulating compatible bindings.

nodes.name: match_triple_pattern
nodes.summary: Resolves a single triple pattern against the store, producing new bindings extending the input binding for each matching triple.

nodes.name: match_simple_triple
nodes.summary: Performs the actual triple-store lookup for a partially resolved pattern, handling subject/predicate/object variable binding combinations.

nodes.name: join_solutions
nodes.summary: Performs an inner join of two solution sequences by merging compatible bindings.

nodes.name: left_join_solutions
nodes.summary: Performs a left outer join (SPARQL OPTIONAL) with an optional filter condition, preserving unmatched left-side bindings.

nodes.name: minus_solutions
nodes.summary: Computes set-difference of solutions, removing left bindings that are compatible with any right binding (SPARQL MINUS semantics).

nodes.name: filter_solutions
nodes.summary: Filters a solution sequence by evaluating the SPARQL filter expression against each binding.

nodes.name: apply_modifiers
nodes.summary: Applies result-set modifiers (ORDER BY, DISTINCT, OFFSET, LIMIT) to the projected solution sequence.

nodes.name: evaluate_expression
nodes.summary: Recursively evaluates a SPARQL expression tree (literals, variables, function calls, arithmetic, logical, comparison) yielding an RDF term.

nodes.name: evaluate_binary_op
nodes.summary: Evaluates binary operators in SPARQL expressions including arithmetic, comparison, and logical operations with type coercion.

nodes.name: execute_construct
nodes.summary: Executes a CONSTRUCT query by evaluating the WHERE pattern and substituting bindings into the template triples.

nodes.name: execute_describe
nodes.summary: Executes a DESCRIBE query, returning all triples mentioning the targeted resources from the store.

nodes.name: SparqlContext
nodes.summary: Execution context bundling the triple store, base IRI, and namespace prefixes for a SPARQL evaluation session.

nodes.name: QueryResult
nodes.summary: Enumeration of possible SPARQL query result kinds (Select, Construct, Ask, Describe, Update).

nodes.name: SelectResult
nodes.summary: Container for SELECT query results holding ordered variable names and the corresponding bindings rows.

nodes.name: id_map.rs
nodes.summary: Bidirectional mapping abstraction between RVF (rvlite-internal) vector IDs and SQL primary keys, with direct and offset-based implementations.
nodes.languageNotes: Trait-based with two implementors: identity (DirectIdMap) and constant-offset (OffsetIdMap) translations.

nodes.name: IdMapping
nodes.summary: Trait defining the contract for translating between RVF vector IDs and SQL primary keys with validation support.

nodes.name: DirectIdMap
nodes.summary: Identity ID mapping where RVF IDs and SQL primary keys are numerically equal, used when both spaces share the same numbering.

nodes.name: OffsetIdMap
nodes.summary: Offset-based ID mapping translating between RVF and SQL keys via a fixed signed offset, used when ID spaces are shifted.

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: Build orchestration for the RVM microhypervisor targeting AArch64 bare-metal, with QEMU run/test/objdump targets and the rvm.ld linker script wired in.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses RUSTFLAGS to inject the linker script and pins the kernel ELF/binary output paths.

nodes.name: entry.rs
nodes.summary: Measured boot entry point that drives the RVM boot sequence through staged phases, hashing each phase into the boot digest chain.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements TPM-style phase digest chaining: digest_n = hash(digest_{n-1} || phase_data).

nodes.name: BootContext
nodes.summary: Boot context aggregating device-tree pointer, accumulated measurement digest, and phase tracking state.

nodes.name: run_boot_sequence
nodes.summary: Drives the full RVM measured boot pipeline through ordered phases (HAL init, memory, capabilities, scheduler, partitions) accumulating phase digests at each step.

nodes.name: phase_digest
nodes.summary: Computes the cumulative SHA-style phase digest by hashing (stage || data) into the rolling boot measurement.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for the RVM capability system implementing the three-layer proof model (P1 check, P2 policy, P3 deferred) per ADR-135, with submodules for derivation, grant, revoke, and verify.
nodes.languageNotes: no_std crate with forbid(unsafe_code) and strict clippy gating; serves as a module barrel re-exporting submodule APIs.

nodes.name: graph.rs
nodes.summary: Coherence graph data structure representing partitions as nodes and communication relationships as weighted edges, with add/remove/update/decay operations used by the scheduler.
nodes.languageNotes: Tracks active nodes/edges and supports weight decay in basis-points for coherence-aware scheduling.

nodes.name: CoherenceGraph
nodes.summary: Main coherence graph holding nodes (partitions), edges (communication channels), and lookup indices for scheduler decisions.

nodes.name: Node
nodes.summary: Graph node representing a partition with active/free status and incident-edge tracking.

nodes.name: Edge
nodes.summary: Directed edge between two partition nodes carrying a weight that quantifies their coherence-affecting communication intensity.

nodes.name: GraphError
nodes.summary: Error enum for coherence-graph operations covering capacity, missing-node, missing-edge, and arithmetic-overflow cases.

nodes.name: add_node
nodes.summary: Adds a partition node, reusing freed slots when available or growing the node vector, enforcing capacity bounds.

nodes.name: remove_node
nodes.summary: Removes a partition node and prunes all incident edges, returning the slot to the free list.

nodes.name: add_edge
nodes.summary: Creates or reuses a directed edge between two partitions and assigns initial weight, enforcing edge-capacity and node-existence invariants.

nodes.name: update_weight
nodes.summary: Applies a signed weight delta to an edge with saturating arithmetic to prevent under/overflow of the communication-weight counter.

nodes.name: internal_weight
nodes.summary: Returns the total weight of edges fully contained within a partition's neighborhood, used by community-detection metrics.

nodes.name: find_directed_edge
nodes.summary: Linear-search lookup for an active edge between two specific partition endpoints in the given direction.

nodes.name: decay_weights
nodes.summary: Multiplicatively decays all edge weights by a basis-point factor each epoch, retiring edges whose weight reaches zero.

nodes.name: remove_edge_by_index
nodes.summary: Removes an edge by its slot index, detaching it from both endpoints' adjacency lists and returning the slot to the edge free list.

nodes.name: NeighborIter
nodes.summary: Iterator over a partition's neighboring partitions reached through its outgoing edges.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: AArch64 HAL barrel module exposing boot, interrupts, MMU, timer, and UART submodules and marking the architecture-specific hardware boundary where unsafe is permitted.

nodes.name: allocator.rs
nodes.summary: Bitmap-backed buddy allocator providing page-sized physical memory allocation/free with coalescing across multiple orders for the RVM kernel.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a per-order free-bit bitmap and supports buddy coalescing in O(log N) on free.

nodes.name: BuddyAllocator
nodes.summary: Buddy memory allocator state holding the base address, per-order free bitmaps, and total managed page count.

nodes.name: new
nodes.summary: Constructs a buddy allocator over a contiguous physical region starting at base, sizing its bitmap to cover all pages and orders.

nodes.name: alloc_pages
nodes.summary: Allocates a 2^order-page block, splitting higher-order free blocks as needed and updating bitmap state.

nodes.name: free_pages
nodes.summary: Returns a 2^order-page block to the allocator and merges it with its buddy when the buddy is also free, walking up the order tree.

nodes.name: coalesce
nodes.summary: Recursive helper that merges a freed block with its buddy if the buddy is also free, promoting the merged region to a higher order.

nodes.name: find_first_free
nodes.summary: Locates the first free block at the given order by scanning the bitmap a machine word at a time using trailing-zero counts.

nodes.name: free_page_count
nodes.summary: Computes the total number of free pages currently tracked by the allocator by summing free blocks weighted by their order size.

nodes.name: ipc.rs
nodes.summary: Inter-partition communication (IPC) implementation providing bounded message queues and a manager that creates/sends/receives/destroys channels gated by capability checks.
nodes.languageNotes: Channels are tied to coherence-graph edges so that IPC traffic updates communication weights used by the scheduler.

nodes.name: IpcMessage
nodes.summary: Fixed-size IPC message payload exchanged between partitions through a channel.

nodes.name: MessageQueue
nodes.summary: Bounded FIFO ring buffer for IPC messages with send, receive, and capacity-status operations.

nodes.name: IpcManager
nodes.summary: Central registry of IPC channels keyed by coherence-graph edge IDs, mediating channel creation, send/receive operations, and destruction.

nodes.name: ChannelMeta
nodes.summary: Metadata for a single IPC channel binding source and destination partition IDs to the coherence edge.

nodes.name: create_channel
nodes.summary: Creates a new IPC channel between two partitions, allocating a coherence-graph edge and registering channel metadata.

nodes.name: send
nodes.summary: Sends a message on a channel after verifying the caller's capability and increments the channel's coherence weight on success.

nodes.name: destroy_channel
nodes.summary: Tears down an IPC channel by removing the associated coherence edge and freeing channel metadata.

nodes.name: constant_time.rs
nodes.summary: Constant-time comparison primitives (32-bit, 64-bit, byte-slice) used in capability proof verification to avoid timing side channels.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses bitwise OR-accumulation and arithmetic-reduction so execution time is independent of input data.

nodes.name: ct_eq
nodes.summary: Constant-time byte-slice equality check producing a boolean without short-circuit branches.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml

nodes.name: switch.rs

nodes.summary: Per-partition saved CPU state used during a context switch, including entry point, stack pointer, VMID, and stage-2 page-table base.

nodes.name: SwitchResult

nodes.name: partition_switch
nodes.summary: Performs the partition context switch by validating the destination, saving the source state, and installing the new register/MMU context.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Initializes a SwitchContext with the partition's entry point, stack pointer, VMID, and stage-2 translation-table base.

nodes.name: addr.rs

nodes.name: PhysAddr
nodes.summary: Physical address newtype with page-alignment query and page-align-down helpers.

nodes.name: VirtAddr
nodes.summary: Virtual address newtype used for hypervisor (EL2) virtual address space.

nodes.name: GuestPhysAddr
nodes.summary: Guest-physical address newtype representing a partition's IPA (intermediate physical address) translated by stage-2 tables.

nodes.name: proof.rs
nodes.summary: Shared proof-system types (ProofTier, ProofResult, ProofToken) consumed across the capability and policy crates.

nodes.name: ProofTier
nodes.summary: Enum of the three proof tiers (P1 capability check, P2 policy validation, P3 deep proof) defined by ADR-135.

nodes.name: ProofResult
nodes.summary: Outcome of a proof check (allow, deny, or pending higher-tier evaluation).

nodes.name: ProofToken
nodes.summary: Opaque token carrying proof evidence used to short-circuit re-evaluation within an epoch.

nodes.name: quota.rs
nodes.summary: Per-partition WASM resource quota tracker enforcing CPU, memory-page, and IPC budgets per epoch with check-and-record helpers.

nodes.name: PartitionQuota
nodes.summary: Configurable per-partition quota budget for CPU microseconds, memory pages, and IPC operations per epoch.

nodes.name: ResourceKind
nodes.summary: Enum identifying the kind of constrained resource (Cpu, Memory, Ipc) tracked by the quota system.

nodes.name: ResourceUsage
nodes.summary: Running tally of per-partition resource consumption matched against the quota budget.

nodes.name: QuotaTracker
nodes.summary: Central tracker holding all partition quotas and usage counters, providing register/check/record/reset operations driven by the scheduler and IPC layers.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Registers a partition with the tracker by associating it with a configured quota budget.

nodes.name: check_quota
nodes.summary: Returns whether adding the requested amount to the partition's usage would stay within the quota budget for the given resource.

nodes.name: record_usage
nodes.summary: Unconditionally records resource consumption against a partition's running usage counter.

nodes.name: check_and_record_cpu
nodes.summary: Atomic check-then-record for CPU-microsecond usage, returning whether the budget allowed the operation.

nodes.name: check_and_record_memory
nodes.summary: Atomic check-then-record for memory-page usage, enforcing the page-budget per partition.

nodes.name: check_and_record_ipc
nodes.summary: Atomic check-then-record for IPC-message usage, enforcing the per-epoch IPC budget.

nodes.name: enforce_quota
nodes.summary: Returns whether any resource budget for a partition is currently exhausted, used by the scheduler to suspend offending partitions.

nodes.name: reset_epoch_counters
nodes.summary: Resets all partition usage counters at the start of a new accounting epoch.

nodes.name: parse_primary_expression

nodes.name: try_parse_function_call

nodes.name: parse_solution_modifiers

nodes.name: parse_inline_values

nodes.name: parse_construct_query

nodes.name: parse_describe_query

nodes.name: parse_update
nodes.summary: Top-level driver for SPARQL UPDATE operations including INSERT, DELETE, MODIFY, LOAD, CREATE, and DROP.

nodes.name: parse_modify_delete

nodes.name: parse_literal

nodes.name: parse_numeric_literal

nodes.name: parse_prefixed_name

nodes.name: BootPhase

nodes.name: BootTracker

nodes.name: complete_phase

nodes.name: RevokeResult

nodes.name: revoke_capability
nodes.summary: Revokes a capability and all capabilities derived from it by walking the derivation tree in the capability table.

nodes.name: MinCutResult

nodes.name: MinCutBridge
nodes.summary: Bridge edge metadata describing an edge that participates in the discovered min-cut.

nodes.name: stoer_wagner_mincut
nodes.summary: Stoer-Wagner global minimum-cut driver that iteratively contracts the graph until a single super-vertex remains.

nodes.name: minimum_cut_phase
nodes.summary: Single phase of the Stoer-Wagner algorithm computing the maximum adjacency ordering and the resulting cut-of-the-phase.

nodes.name: build_result
nodes.summary: Constructs the MinCutResult struct from the internal partition state once the algorithm finishes.

nodes.name: uart_init
nodes.summary: Initializes the PL011 UART by configuring baud rate, line control, and enabling the transmitter/receiver.

nodes.summary: Writes a single byte to the UART data register, busy-waiting on the TX FIFO until space is available.

nodes.name: uart_put_hex
nodes.summary: Emits a 64-bit value to the UART as zero-padded hexadecimal digits.

nodes.name: uart_put_hex32

nodes.name: CheckpointId
nodes.summary: Newtype wrapper for u64 checkpoint identifiers used by the reconstruction pipeline.

nodes.name: WitnessDelta
nodes.summary: Structured memory delta captured by the witness chain, replayed during reconstruction.

nodes.name: CompressedCheckpoint
nodes.summary: Compressed checkpoint payload bundling the encoded state, hash, and version metadata.

nodes.name: ReconstructionResult

nodes.name: ReconstructionPipeline

nodes.name: add_delta

nodes.name: reconstruct

nodes.name: create_checkpoint
nodes.summary: Snapshots the current state into a compressed, hashed checkpoint that can later be replayed against.

nodes.name: compress

nodes.name: decompress

nodes.name: fnv1a_hash

nodes.name: valid_transition

nodes.name: verify_and_witness

nodes.name: verify_p3

nodes.name: verify_p3_signed

nodes.name: emit_proof_witness

nodes.name: emit_proof_rejected

nodes.name: emit_signed_proof_witness

nodes.name: emit_signed_proof_rejected
nodes.summary: Emits a rejection event for a signed proof that failed verification.

nodes.name: DegradedState

nodes.name: CoherenceScore

nodes.name: PhiValue

nodes.name: CutPressure

nodes.name: CommEdgeId

nodes.name: CommEdge

nodes.name: SchedulerMode

nodes.name: Priority

nodes.name: EpochConfig

nodes.name: graph_store.rs
nodes.summary: In-memory property graph store backing the Cypher engine, providing typed Value, Node, Edge, and PropertyGraph types with label and edge-type indices plus CRUD operations.
nodes.languageNotes: Hand-rolled adjacency lists with separate outgoing/incoming indices for O(1) traversal.

nodes.name: Value
nodes.summary: Tagged enum representing primitive and composite property values (Null, Boolean, Integer, Float, String, List, Map) with as_* accessor helpers and From conversions.

nodes.name: Node
nodes.summary: Property-graph node with id, labels, and property map plus builder/accessor methods to manage labels and property values.

nodes.name: Edge
nodes.summary: Directed property-graph edge connecting two node ids with a typed label and arbitrary properties.

nodes.name: PropertyGraph
nodes.summary: Core property graph container holding node and edge maps, label and edge-type indices, and incoming/outgoing adjacency lists with monotonically increasing id generators.

nodes.name: add_node
nodes.summary: Inserts a node into the graph and updates the label index entries for each of the node's labels.

nodes.name: add_edge
nodes.summary: Inserts an edge after verifying both endpoint nodes exist, and updates edge-type, outgoing, and incoming indices.

nodes.name: delete_node
nodes.summary: Removes a node and cascades the deletion across label index, outgoing/incoming edge maps, and the affected edges themselves.

nodes.name: delete_edge
nodes.summary: Removes an edge and prunes its id from the edge-type, outgoing, and incoming indices.

nodes.name: triple_store.rs
nodes.summary: Thread-safe RDF triple store with SPO/POS/OSP indexes, named-graph support, and pattern-matching query for the rvlite SPARQL engine.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses RwLock-guarded HashMaps and an AtomicU64 id counter for safe concurrent reads with exclusive writes.

nodes.name: TripleStore
nodes.summary: Concurrent RDF triple store maintaining triple records, SPO/POS/OSP indices, named graphs, subject/predicate/object dictionaries, and a monotonically increasing triple id.

nodes.name: insert_into_graph
nodes.summary: Allocates a new triple id, updates SPO/POS/OSP indices, term dictionaries, and tracks membership in either a named graph or the default graph.

nodes.name: query_with_graph
nodes.summary: Pattern-match query selecting the optimal index (SPO/POS/OSP) for any combination of bound and unbound subject, predicate, object, then filters by named or default graph membership.

nodes.name: clear
nodes.summary: Resets the triple store by clearing the triple table, all three indices, named graphs, default graph, and term dictionaries.

nodes.name: clear_graph
nodes.summary: Removes all triples belonging to a specific named graph while leaving other graphs untouched.

nodes.name: remove
nodes.summary: Deletes a triple by id and reclaims its entries from SPO/POS/OSP indices, named-graph membership sets, and the default graph.

nodes.name: stats
nodes.summary: Snapshots the current store statistics (triple count, unique subject/predicate/object counts, graph count) for instrumentation.

nodes.name: get_graph
nodes.summary: Returns all triples in a named graph by joining graph membership ids with the triple table.

nodes.name: term_to_key
nodes.summary: Serialization helper that normalizes an RDF term (IRI, blank node, literal, variable) into a canonical string key for index lookups.

nodes.name: state.rs
nodes.summary: Serializable state types representing the entire rvlite database snapshot, covering vector entries, graph nodes/edges, RDF triples, and SQL tables for persistence and IndexedDB hand-off.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Short README describing the criterion-based benchmark suite for rvm crates.

nodes.name: measured.rs
nodes.summary: Maintains the cumulative measured-boot hash chain used to attest the integrity of each boot stage in rvm.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses no_std-friendly fixed-size byte arrays for measurement digests.

nodes.name: MeasuredBootState
nodes.summary: Running measured-boot state tracking the current accumulator and per-stage measurements as the bootloader progresses.

nodes.name: extend_measurement
nodes.summary: Folds a new stage measurement into the running boot hash to produce the next attested measurement value.

nodes.name: table.rs
nodes.summary: Capability table implementation with slot-based storage, generation counters to prevent use-after-free, and derivation/lookup APIs for the rvm capability system.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses generation-tagged slots so revoked capabilities cannot be aliased by later insertions.

nodes.name: CapSlot
nodes.summary: Single entry in the capability table tracking generation, capability payload, and derivation lineage for revocation safety.

nodes.name: insert_root
nodes.summary: Inserts a new root capability with a fresh generation, returning a typed handle for later lookup.

nodes.name: insert_derived
nodes.summary: Derives a child capability from an existing parent slot, recording lineage so revocation propagates correctly.

nodes.name: lookup
nodes.summary: Resolves a capability handle to its slot, validating the generation tag to reject stale handles.

nodes.name: remove
nodes.summary: Revokes a capability by bumping the slot generation and clearing the payload while preserving the slot for reuse.

nodes.name: find_free_slot
nodes.summary: Scans the slot vector for an empty entry, returning its index or extending the table when none are free.

nodes.name: pressure.rs
nodes.summary: Computes cut pressure across the partition coherence graph and evaluates merge candidates to balance partition load in rvm.

nodes.name: compute_cut_pressure
nodes.summary: Computes the aggregate cross-partition edge weight to estimate the cost of the current partition cut.

nodes.name: evaluate_merge
nodes.summary: Scores a candidate merge of two partitions against the current cut pressure, returning a delta used by the partition manager to decide consolidation.

nodes.name: lib.rs

nodes.languageNotes: no_std crate that re-exports an aarch64 backend module when targeted at ARM64.

nodes.name: Platform
nodes.summary: Top-level platform trait exposing CPU identification and architecture detection hooks for the rvm HAL.

nodes.name: MmuOps
nodes.summary: MMU operation trait covering page-table installation, TLB invalidation, and virtual-to-physical translation hooks for the HAL.

nodes.name: TimerOps
nodes.summary: Timer trait exposing now/deadline scheduling primitives so the kernel scheduler stays architecture-neutral.

nodes.name: InterruptOps
nodes.summary: Interrupt control trait wrapping mask, unmask, and dispatch entry points so platform-specific GIC/PLIC drivers can plug into the kernel.

nodes.name: region.rs

nodes.languageNotes: Manages tiered memory backing via the sibling tier module and enforces single-owner invariants on every transfer.

nodes.name: OwnedRegion
nodes.summary: A memory region owned by exactly one partition, recording base/length, tier classification, and the controlling partition id.

nodes.name: create
nodes.summary: Allocates a new owned memory region within a specified tier and partition, validating bounds, alignment, and tier capacity before recording the assignment.

nodes.name: create_auto_id
nodes.summary: Convenience constructor that allocates an owned region with an auto-generated region id.

nodes.name: destroy
nodes.summary: Releases an owned region back to its tier and removes it from the partition's ownership map.

nodes.name: transfer
nodes.summary: Hands a region from one partition to another atomically, ensuring single-owner invariants and updating tier metadata.

nodes.name: translate
nodes.summary: Resolves a virtual address within a region to its underlying physical/tier-relative offset for paging and direct access paths.

nodes.name: regions_for_partition
nodes.summary: Lists every region currently owned by a given partition for inspection and reclamation purposes.

nodes.name: manager.rs
nodes.summary: PartitionManager coordinating lifecycle of partitions, providing typed CRUD operations and id allocation for the rvm partitioning subsystem.

nodes.name: PartitionManager
nodes.summary: Central registry tracking active partitions, their state, and id allocation for the rvm runtime.

nodes.name: create
nodes.summary: Creates a new partition with the supplied configuration, allocating a fresh id and registering it in the manager.

nodes.name: get
nodes.summary: Resolves a partition handle to an immutable reference, returning an error when the id is unknown.

nodes.name: get_mut
nodes.summary: Resolves a partition handle to a mutable reference for state-mutating operations.

nodes.name: remove
nodes.summary: Removes a partition by id, freeing its resources and cleaning up registry entries.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for the rvm-proof TEE proof system, re-exporting constant-time helpers, signing/verifier abstractions, policy engine, and TEE provider integration.

nodes.name: Proof
nodes.summary: Public Proof record bundling a hash, signature, and payload metadata produced by the rvm proof engine for verification.

nodes.name: verify
nodes.summary: Verifies a proof by validating hash binding, signature, and TEE attestation policy in constant time to prevent timing leaks.

nodes.name: epoch.rs
nodes.summary: Epoch tracker that advances monotonically with each scheduler tick and exposes the current epoch to other rvm subsystems.

nodes.name: EpochTracker
nodes.summary: Atomic epoch counter providing tick advancement, current epoch readout, and quiescence helpers for the scheduler.

nodes.name: attestation.rs
nodes.summary: Attestation chain that records boot, capability, and partition events into an append-only hash-linked log for remote verification.

nodes.name: AttestationChain
nodes.summary: Append-only attestation log linking each entry to the previous hash so any tampering with prior events is detectable.

nodes.name: add_entry
nodes.summary: Appends a new attestation entry, folding it into the running hash chain to extend the audit trail.

nodes.name: extend_chain_hash
nodes.summary: Computes the next attestation hash by folding the prior hash with the new entry payload using a deterministic digest.

nodes.name: config.rs
nodes.summary: Shared configuration value types used by other rvm crates to thread runtime tunables through the system.

nodes.name: witness.rs
nodes.summary: Witness record types and action kinds describing rvm runtime events plus an FNV-1a hash helper used to digest witness payloads.

nodes.name: WitnessRecord
nodes.summary: Single witness event capturing action kind, actor, target, timestamp, and payload digest for the rvm audit log.

nodes.name: WitnessHash
nodes.summary: Newtype wrapping a fixed-size digest array with zeroed-default and conversion helpers for compact witness records.

nodes.name: ActionKind
nodes.summary: Enum enumerating every recordable rvm action (partition lifecycle, capability grants/revokes, memory maps, proof rejection) along with their payload shapes.

nodes.name: emit.rs
nodes.summary: Helper functions that emit structured witness records for partition, capability, memory, and proof events into the rvm witness log.

nodes.name: emit_partition_create
nodes.summary: Appends a partition-create witness record describing the new partition's identity and configuration.

nodes.name: emit_partition_destroy
nodes.summary: Records a witness entry for partition destruction, capturing the partition id and timestamp.

nodes.name: emit_capability_grant
nodes.summary: Logs a capability grant event to the witness chain, recording grantor, grantee, and rights.

nodes.name: emit_capability_revoke
nodes.summary: Logs a capability revocation event, including the revoked handle and revoking subject.

nodes.name: emit_memory_map
nodes.summary: Records a memory-mapping action with the originating partition, region range, and tier classification.

nodes.name: emit_proof_rejected
nodes.summary: Captures a proof-rejection event into the witness log, attaching the failure reason for forensic analysis.

nodes.name: Lexer
nodes.summary: Streaming character-level Cypher lexer that tracks position information and emits a sequence of typed tokens with lookahead support.

nodes.name: TokenKind
nodes.summary: Enum enumerating every Cypher token kind: keywords, identifiers, literals, operators, punctuation, and end-of-input.

nodes.name: next_token
nodes.summary: Main lexer dispatch that consumes whitespace then advances over the next token, branching by character class into the appropriate scan routine.

nodes.name: scan_identifier
nodes.summary: Reads an identifier or quoted-identifier from the input stream and disambiguates against the Cypher keyword set.

nodes.name: scan_number
nodes.summary: Scans an integer or floating-point numeric literal, handling optional sign, decimal point, and exponent suffix.

nodes.name: scan_string
nodes.summary: Scans a single- or double-quoted string literal with escape handling and produces a StringLit token.

nodes.name: tokenize
nodes.summary: Convenience entry-point that drives a Lexer to completion and returns the full token vector for a Cypher source string.

nodes.name: ast.rs
nodes.summary: Abstract syntax tree definitions for the rvlite SQL dialect, covering CREATE TABLE / INSERT / SELECT with vector distance and ORDER BY / LIMIT extensions.

nodes.name: SqlStatement
nodes.summary: Top-level SQL statement enum with CreateTable, Insert, and Select variants used as the parser output type.

nodes.name: Expression

nodes.name: Value
nodes.summary: Polymorphic SQL value with JSON serialization helpers used both at parse time and during execution.

nodes.name: writer_lease.rs
nodes.summary: File-based cooperative writer lease for rvlite stores, providing PID/heartbeat lock files with staleness detection to evict crashed writers.

nodes.name: WriterLease
nodes.summary: RAII handle representing an exclusive writer lock on a rvlite store path, automatically releasing the lock on drop.

nodes.name: acquire
nodes.summary: Attempts to acquire an exclusive writer lease on a store path, taking over stale locks whose heartbeat exceeds the threshold.

nodes.name: is_stale
nodes.summary: Determines whether a peer lease should be considered abandoned based on heartbeat age and process liveness checks.

nodes.name: refresh_heartbeat
nodes.summary: Rewrites the lease lock file with the current timestamp so concurrent writers can see the lease is still alive.

nodes.name: is_pid_alive
nodes.summary: OS-level liveness probe that calls kill(pid, 0) via libc to test whether the previous lease holder is still running.

nodes.name: try_create_lock
nodes.summary: Helper that creates the lock file with O_EXCL semantics and writes the lease metadata, returning AlreadyExists if another writer holds it.

nodes.name: coherence.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion micro-benchmark measuring the per-call cost of EmaFilter::update in the coherence scoring pipeline.

nodes.name: sequence.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the 7-phase deterministic boot sequence specified in ADR-137, with forward-only stage transitions, timing capture, and witness emission per stage.

nodes.name: BootStage
nodes.summary: Enum encoding the seven ordered boot phases from ResetVector through SchedulerEntry.

nodes.name: BootSequence
nodes.summary: Stateful driver that walks the boot phases forward-only, records timing per phase, and accumulates a witness digest summarizing the boot.

nodes.name: complete_stage
nodes.summary: Marks the current boot phase complete, records its duration, updates the witness digest, and advances to the next stage.

nodes.name: begin_stage
nodes.summary: Starts timing the given boot phase, rejecting attempts to skip or rewind through stages.

nodes.name: verify.rs
nodes.summary: Three-layer proof verifier (P1 existence, P2 invariants, P3 derivation chain) for capabilities, with nonce-ring replay prevention (ADR-135).

nodes.name: ProofVerifier
nodes.summary: Stateful verifier holding the nonce replay ring, current epoch, and toggles for relaxed verification used by P1/P2/P3 paths.

nodes.name: PolicyContext
nodes.summary: Bag of expected-owner, region bounds, lease expiry, and current time inputs supplied to P2 invariant checks.

nodes.name: verify_p3
nodes.summary: Performs the deepest proof check: walks the derivation chain to ensure root reachability, epoch monotonicity, and unbroken rights propagation.

nodes.name: verify_p2
nodes.summary: Constant-time structural invariant validation against PolicyContext: owner match, region bounds, lease validity.

nodes.name: verify_p1
nodes.summary: Fast capability existence and rights check using bitmap AND against the capability table.

nodes.name: mark_nonce
nodes.summary: Records a freshly verified nonce in the 4096-slot replay ring buffer, evicting the oldest entry on overflow.

nodes.name: check_nonce
nodes.summary: Tests whether a nonce has already been seen in the replay ring buffer to reject duplicate proofs.

nodes.name: scoring.rs
nodes.summary: Fixed-point coherence score computation expressing intra-partition edge weight as a fraction of total edge weight in basis points (0..10000).

nodes.name: PartitionCoherenceResult
nodes.summary: Result tuple carrying the partition id, score, internal edge weight, and total edge weight produced by coherence scoring.

nodes.name: compute_coherence_score
nodes.summary: Computes the basis-point coherence score for a single partition by summing internal and external edge weights and taking their ratio.

nodes.name: recompute_all_scores
nodes.summary: Iterates over every partition in the coherence graph and returns a PartitionCoherenceResult for each.

nodes.name: Tier

nodes.name: TierThresholds

nodes.name: RegionTierState

nodes.name: TierManager
nodes.summary: Owner of all region tier state, advancing epochs and performing promotion / demotion / decay according to ADR-136.

nodes.name: promote
nodes.summary: Promotes a region one tier up when its residency score exceeds the promotion threshold, updating tier and timestamps.

nodes.name: demote
nodes.summary: Demotes a region one tier down when residency falls below the eviction threshold.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Inserts a new region into the tier manager at the requested starting tier, allocating a slot and initializing state.

nodes.name: find_demotion_candidates
nodes.summary: Scans all regions to surface those whose residency score has dropped enough to be eligible for demotion.

nodes.name: update_cut_value
nodes.summary: Updates the partition cut value contribution stored in a region's tier state.

nodes.name: record_access
nodes.summary: Records an access event by bumping recency for the region and updating last-access epoch.

nodes.name: unregister
nodes.summary: Removes a region from the tier manager and frees its slot.

nodes.name: merge.rs
nodes.summary: Partition merge precondition checks (DC-11): coherence threshold, graph adjacency, and combined resource limit enforcement.

nodes.name: merge_preconditions_full
nodes.summary: Runs the full DC-11 precondition triple (coherence, adjacency, resource cap) and returns the first failed reason.

nodes.name: merge_preconditions_met
nodes.summary: Boolean shortcut over merge_preconditions_full for callers that only need a yes/no answer.

nodes.name: policy.rs
nodes.summary: Constant-time P2 policy rule evaluator covering ownership, region bounds, lease expiry, delegation depth, nonce replay, and time window with side-channel resistance.

nodes.name: Rule
nodes.summary: Enum of P2 rule kinds: OwnershipChain, RegionBounds, LeaseExpiry, DelegationDepth, NonceReplay, TimeWindow.

nodes.name: PolicyEvaluator
nodes.summary: Stateful evaluator that holds a nonce replay ring and runs every P2 rule in constant time regardless of outcome.

nodes.name: evaluate_rule
nodes.summary: Dispatch over Rule variants and evaluate the corresponding check in constant time, returning a typed RvmError on failure.

nodes.name: evaluate_all_rules
nodes.summary: Runs every P2 rule even if earlier ones fail, accumulating the first failure to prevent timing side channels.

nodes.name: is_nonce_replayed
nodes.summary: Constant-time lookup of a candidate nonce against the 4096-slot replay ring.

nodes.name: record_nonce
nodes.summary: Inserts a verified nonce into the replay ring, advancing the head pointer modulo the ring size.

nodes.summary: Per-epoch DMA bandwidth budget tracking max bytes and bytes-used, with check/reset helpers.

nodes.name: ResourceQuota
nodes.summary: Combined CPU-time, memory, and IPC quota with per-epoch reset and exhaustion checks.

nodes.name: check_dma
nodes.summary: Verifies a requested DMA transfer fits in remaining budget and debits it, returning RvmError::BudgetExceeded otherwise.

nodes.name: check_cpu_time
nodes.summary: Charges CPU time against the quota and rejects allocations once the per-epoch budget is exhausted.

nodes.name: check_memory
nodes.summary: Reserves a memory allocation against the quota and rejects overages.

nodes.name: check_ipc
nodes.summary: Debits an IPC message from the quota's per-epoch rate budget.

nodes.name: reset_epoch
nodes.summary: Resets per-epoch counters (CPU, IPC, DMA) at epoch boundaries while preserving long-lived memory allocations.

nodes.name: DualHmacSigner
nodes.summary: Strong symmetric WitnessSigner producing 64-byte signatures by chaining two independent HMAC-SHA256 instances for additional resistance to single-key compromise.

nodes.name: NullSigner

tour.title: Slice 6c: rvm Microhypervisor Overview
tour.description: Start with the rvm crate-level README, the architecture roadmap doc, and the workspace Cargo.toml. rvm is a coherence-native, no_std AArch64 microhypervisor whose workspace stitches 13 internal crates (types, hal, cap, witness, proof, partition, sched, memory, coherence, boot, wasm, security, kernel) into a single hypervisor image. These three artifacts establish the partition-based architecture, the capability/witness/proof trust chain, and the crate map you will walk through over the next several steps.

tour.title: Slice 6c: Bare-Metal Boot & AArch64 HAL
tour.description: Bring-up begins with rvm-boot's measured boot tracker, the entry-point that hashes each phase into a boot digest chain, and the rvm-hal AArch64 barrel that exposes boot, MMU, timer, interrupts, and UART submodules. This is the only place in the hypervisor where unsafe is permitted — every architecture-specific MSR write, MMU table setup, and exception-vector install lives behind this HAL boundary. The Makefile and QEMU targets later in the tour run exactly this entry point on virt machines.
tour.languageLesson: no_std Rust crates omit the standard library and target bare-metal environments. Symbols normally provided by std (panic handler, allocator, eh_personality) must be supplied by the binary or a downstream crate, which is why rvm-boot, rvm-hal and friends compose into rvm-kernel rather than running standalone.

tour.title: Slice 6c: rvm-types Domain Vocabulary
tour.description: rvm-types is the shared type-system crate every other rvm crate imports from. Its lib.rs re-exports addr, capability, coherence, config, device, error, ids, memory, partition, proof, recovery, scheduler, and witness modules so that PartitionId, CapabilityId, MemoryRegion, and the various error enums are defined in exactly one place. Understanding this vocabulary up front makes every subsequent crate's API readable at a glance.

tour.title: Slice 6c: Capability System (ADR-135 P1)
tour.description: rvm-cap implements the first layer (P1) of the three-layer proof model from ADR-135. The capability table uses slot-based storage with generation counters to prevent use-after-free, and the derivation tree tracks parent/child relationships so revoking a parent atomically revokes every descendant. Every privileged operation in the hypervisor must present a valid capability before any policy or proof checking even runs.
tour.languageLesson: Generation counters are the no_std analogue of weak references: each slot pairs a payload with a monotonically-increasing counter, and capability handles store the generation they were minted at. Lookups that don't match the current generation return CapabilityRevoked instead of dereferencing a recycled slot, preventing use-after-free without needing an allocator-backed reference count.

tour.title: Slice 6c: Partition Object Model (ADR-133)
tour.description: rvm-partition defines the Partition aggregate per ADR-133 — lifecycle states, partition-type taxonomy, local cut pressure, and the IpcManager / CommEdge types that model inter-partition communication. The crate root re-exports the CapabilityTable from Step 4 alongside PartitionManager, making clear that partitions are the unit of isolation, capability scoping, and coherence-graph membership. Cut pressure recorded here feeds the coherence engine two steps later.

tour.title: Slice 6c: Four-Tier Memory & Reconstruction (ADR-136/137)
tour.description: rvm-memory layers a Hot/Warm/Dormant/Cold tier manager (ADR-136) on top of region allocation and permission tracking. Tiering decisions are driven by coherence cut-value plus recency, so memory follows the same signal that drives scheduling. The reconstruction module (ADR-137) checkpoints partition memory and replays witness-delta logs to rebuild state after a fault, tying memory directly into the witness logging subsystem two steps ahead.

tour.title: Slice 6c: Stoer-Wagner Coherence Engine (ADR-132)
tour.description: rvm-coherence is the conceptual heart of the slice. The coherence graph treats partitions as nodes and communication frequency as weighted edges; the Stoer-Wagner min-cut implementation finds the cheapest split each epoch, and the CoherenceEngine combines that with adaptive pressure analysis to recommend merge/split/migrate decisions to the scheduler. This is what makes rvm coherence-native rather than priority-native.
tour.languageLesson: Stoer-Wagner finds a global minimum cut of an undirected weighted graph in O(V*E + V^2 log V) without any explicit source/sink. Each phase performs a maximum-adjacency ordering, records the last-added vertex as one side of a candidate cut, and contracts it with the previous vertex; the cheapest candidate across all phases is the answer. For a hypervisor scheduler this means 'the natural fault line between hot communication clusters' falls out of the algorithm without operator hints.

tour.title: Slice 6c: Coherence-Gated Scheduler (ADR-132 DC-4)
tour.description: rvm-sched consumes the cut decisions from Step 7 and translates them into per-CPU scheduling under SMP. The crate root wires modes, priority, per-CPU state, epoch boundaries, context-switch primitives, and a degraded fallback path for when the coherence engine itself is unavailable. Together with rvm-coherence, this implements design choice DC-4 from ADR-132: the scheduler is downstream of coherence, not parallel to it.

tour.title: Slice 6c: Witness Log (ADR-134)
tour.description: rvm-witness is the append-only audit substrate the rest of the hypervisor writes through. ADR-134 fixes the 64-byte record format and FNV-1a hash chain so the log is tamper-evident even without external signing; the crate also exposes emitter, log, replay queries, and pluggable signer implementations. Every privileged decision the security gate makes in Step 11 leaves a witness record here.

tour.title: Slice 6c: TEE Proof Engine (ADR-142 P3)
tour.description: rvm-proof is the deferred (P3) verification layer of the ADR-135 proof model and the realisation of ADR-142 phase 2. The signer trait abstracts over HMAC-SHA256, Ed25519, dual-HMAC and Null implementations so witnesses can be authenticated by whichever crypto a board ships with, and the engine verifies plain, capability-bound, and signed proof witnesses, emitting structured proof-witness or proof-rejected events. This crate is where the TEE provider integration lives.

tour.title: Slice 6c: SecurityGate (P1+P2+P3 Funnel)
tour.description: rvm-security fuses the previous three steps into one thing — a SecurityGate that funnels every privileged hypercall through P1 capability check, P2 policy decision, and optional P3 chain verification, emitting a witness record on both allow and reject paths. This is the single chokepoint where capability, policy, and proof become indivisible: bypassing any one of them requires bypassing the gate, and the gate is the only API the kernel exposes to guests.

tour.title: Slice 6c: Per-Partition WASM Guests (ADR-140)
tour.description: rvm-wasm runs untrusted guest code as WebAssembly modules inside each partition. validate_module performs a header + LEB128 scan to reject malformed modules before load; the AgentManager implements the ADR-140 badge-derived AgentId and state machine (Initializing/Running/Suspended/Migrating/Hibernated/Reconstructing/Terminated) with every transition logged through the witness subsystem from Step 9. The quota module enforces per-epoch CPU, memory-page, and IPC budgets so a runaway agent can't starve its neighbours.
tour.languageLesson: LEB128 (Little-Endian Base 128) is the variable-length integer encoding used throughout the WebAssembly binary format: each byte contributes 7 bits of payload with the top bit acting as a continuation flag. A safe LEB128 decoder bounds the byte count (a u32 needs at most 5 bytes, a u64 at most 10) to defeat denial-of-service via runaway varints.

tour.title: Slice 6c: rvm-kernel Integration & QEMU Bring-Up
tour.description: rvm-kernel is the integration capstone — it wires HAL, capabilities, witness, proof, partitions, scheduler, memory, coherence, boot, WASM, and security into a single Kernel API consumed by the boot entry from Step 2. The Makefile drives the bare-metal build with the rvm.ld linker script and runs the image under QEMU virt for tests, objdump, and interactive bring-up, while the GitHub Actions workflow keeps cargo check / test green on every push.
tour.languageLesson: A linker script (rvm.ld) tells the linker exactly where each section (.text, .rodata, .data, .bss, stack, vector table) lives in physical memory. For bare-metal AArch64 you typically place the entry point at a fixed load address (often 0x40080000 for QEMU virt), align the exception vector table to 0x800, and reserve a BSS region the boot code can zero before Rust takes over.

tour.title: Slice 6c: rvlite — WASM-Shipped Lite Database
tour.description: Pivot now to the second half of the slice. rvlite is a standalone vector + triple store derived from RuVector but built cdylib+rlib so it compiles to WASM for browser, edge, and mobile targets without requiring Postgres. lib.rs is the public surface — it owns configuration, error types, and the dispatcher that routes SQL, Cypher, and SPARQL queries to the per-engine subsystems we explore next. The storage/epoch module reconciles RVF persistence epochs with metadata on open, picking rollback / replay / reconcile actions.

tour.title: Slice 6c: SQL / Cypher / SPARQL Tri-Engine
tour.description: rvlite ships three query engines side by side. The SQL parser is a hand-written tokenizer + recursive-descent parser handling SELECT/INSERT/CREATE/DROP plus vector literals. The Cypher subsystem exposes a wasm-bindgen CypherEngine wrapping lexer/parser/executor over an in-memory property graph and bridging to persisted state. The SPARQL executor evaluates SELECT/CONSTRUCT/ASK/DESCRIBE/UPDATE against a triple store with full BGP matching, joins, filters and aggregates — all dispatched from the lib.rs surface in Step 14.

tour.title: Slice 6c: rvlite Dashboard Demo (WASM in the Browser)
tour.description: The capstone is the dashboard example — a single-file React SPA (App.tsx, 4000+ lines) that loads the rvlite WASM build directly in the browser and orchestrates SQL/SPARQL/Cypher consoles, metrics panels, a filter builder, and a learning-engine integration. It is the user-facing proof that the same tri-engine from Step 15 runs unchanged outside any server. The Tailwind + Vite tooling shows the recommended packaging path for downstream rvlite consumers.
tour.languageLesson: wasm-bindgen + cdylib lets a Rust crate compile to a .wasm artifact paired with auto-generated JS glue that marshals strings, objects, and typed arrays across the JS/WASM boundary. Loading is usually async (init() returns a Promise) because browsers stream-compile the module; once initialised, exported Rust functions look like ordinary async JS functions, which is exactly how App.tsx talks to rvlite.
